title: malus orospedanus
intro: Malus orospedanus, also known as the Wild Apple or Golden Pippin, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family, native to the Himalayan region. The bark, leaves, and flowers of Malus orospedanus have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant's health benefits include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and treating gastrointestinal problems. In traditional herbal preparations, the bark and leaves are often made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, while the flowers are used in perfumes and as a natural dye.
